title: Calathea myrosma
taxonomy: Calathea myrosma is a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae. It is a member of the genus Calathea, which is a group of plants commonly known as the peacock plants. The Marantaceae family is a group of flowering plants native to the tropical regions of the Americas, Africa, and Asia. This family is characterized by its distinctive inflorescence and often brightly colored leaves. The classification of Calathea myrosma is further divided into several subspecies, each with its unique characteristics.

intro: Calathea myrosma, also known as Myrosma or Myrosma calathea, is a tropical plant species within the Marantaceae family, native to Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its potential to alleviate respiratory issues and act as an expectorant, helping to relieve coughs and congestion. The bioactive constituents of Calathea myrosma include saponins and flavonoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Calathea myrosma is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma.
